items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit Record Closing Highs
items.content: S&P 500 gained 0.58% to close at 7,563.63. Nasdaq Composite rose 0.91% to 26,917.47. Dow Jones added 0.05% to 50,668.97. Records driven by US-Iran ceasefire hopes and strong earnings reports.

items.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Extension for 60 Days Under Discussion
items.content: Reports indicate potential agreement between US and Iran to extend ceasefire for 60 days, driving risk-on sentiment in equity markets despite continued tensions in Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: April PCE Inflation Rises to 3.8% YoY, Fastest in Three Years
items.content: Personal Consumption Expenditures index shows inflation accelerating to 3.8% year-on-year, driven by higher energy prices. Market nevertheless rallied on the data.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Falls 1.27% to 25,006, Weighed by Geopolitical Concerns
items.content: Hong Kong market declined despite AI optimism. Renewed US airstrikes in Strait of Hormuz raised energy supply concerns. Index has corrected ~11% from YTD high of ~28,000.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge as Strait of Hormuz Disruptions Continue
items.content: WTI crude touched $150/bbl amid severe shipping disruptions through Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude exceeding $100/bbl. Analysts warn era of cheap oil ($60) is over.

items.title: Gold Pulls Back From January All-Time High Despite Geopolitical Tensions
items.content: Gold prices corrected lower from January ATH, pressured by strong US dollar and higher-for-longer interest rate expectations. Structural demand from central banks provides medium-term support.

items.title: China's 15th Five-Year Plan Prioritizes Tech Self-Reliance and AI
items.content: GDP growth target set at 4.5-5.0%. Fiscal deficit at 4% of GDP. Major investment directed toward AI, semiconductors, 6G. NEV sector consolidation expected.

items.title: Strong Earnings Surge: Dollar Tree +18%, Best Buy +15.8%, Snowflake +36%
items.content: Corporate earnings season continues to impress. Snowflake surged 36% after strong Q1 results and major Amazon deal. Profit margins hit record highs in IT sector driven by AI capex.

items.title: Treasury Yields Ease: 10-Year Falls to 4.45%
items.content: US Treasury yields eased as oil prices gave back earlier gains. 10-year yield fell from 4.48% to 4.45%, providing relief to rate-sensitive sectors.

items.title: Commodity Prices Projected to Rise 16% in 2026, Fertilizer +31%
items.content: Overall commodity prices see notable increase driven by energy and fertilizers. Base metals (aluminum, copper) expected to reach all-time highs. Critical minerals gain prominence.

items.title: Hang Seng Range-Bound for 7 Weeks, Struggling to Establish Trend
items.content: HSI trading between 25,300-26,850 for over 7 weeks. Chinese crackdown on offshore brokers and key tech earnings weigh on sentiment. Xiaomi impacted by global memory chip shortage.

items.title: Stagflation Concerns Rise as Inflation Persists Amid Slowing Growth
items.content: Economists warn of stagflationary shock — high inflation combined with stagnant growth. Wealth effect from AI stock gains could fuel further consumer-led inflation.

items.title: China's Policy Pivot: From Debt-Driven Growth to High-Quality Development
items.content: 2026 marks formal shift under 15th Five-Year Plan. Priorities: industrial consolidation, green energy, smart infrastructure, low-altitude economy (drones, eVTOL).

items.title: Defense Sector Surges on Middle East Conflicts
items.content: Defense contractors seeing surge in demand and earnings driven by ongoing Middle East conflicts. AI-related defense spending also accelerating.

items.title: AI Capex Boom Reshaping Markets, Micron Market Cap Surges
items.content: AI-related capital spending driving record profit margins in IT sector. Tech startups attracting substantial funding for data center development. AI benefits not evenly distributed.
